      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I use V-Sync with G-Sync/FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "In NVIDIA Control Panel: Turn ON (acts as frame cap at top of VRR range) In-game: Turn OFF (prevents double-buffering lag) This combination gives you the best of both worlds: tear-free gaming within VRR range and smooth capping at max refresh."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the best response time setting?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Start at Medium/Normal, then test with testufo.com/ghosting. Increase if you see ghosting trails. Decrease if you see inverse ghosting (bright coronas). The \"1ms\" advertised often requires extreme overdrive that causes issues."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I use fullscreen or borderless window?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Competitive games: Exclusive fullscreen (lowest input lag) Casual/multitasking: Borderless windowed (alt-tab faster) Note: Windows 11 optimizations have reduced the difference significantly."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I know if my settings are working?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Use these tests: - Refresh rate: testufo.com (shows actual Hz) - G-Sync/FreeSync: NVIDIA Pendulum or VRR indicator in monitor OSD - Response time: testufo.com/ghosting - Input lag: Feel for responsiveness, or use NVIDIA Reflex"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is HDR worth enabling?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes if: - Your monitor is HDR600 or better - Game has good HDR support - You've calibrated HDR settings No if: - Monitor is only HDR400 - HDR looks washed out - Game has poor HDR implementation ---"
      }
    },
    {

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I know what resolution my monitor supports?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Check your monitor's specifications in the manual or manufacturer website. Most monitors display their native resolution in the product name (e.g., \"24-inch 1080p Monitor\"). You can also check in Windows Display Settings or Mac System Preferences where all supported resolutions are listed in the dropdown menu."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between resolution and screen size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Resolution is the number of pixels (e.g., 1920×1080), while screen size is the physical diagonal measurement in inches (e.g., 24\"). The same resolution can appear on different sized screens - a 1080p laptop screen looks much sharper than a 1080p TV because the pixels are packed more densely (higher PPI)."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I use the highest resolution my monitor supports?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generally yes, use your monitor's native resolution for the sharpest image. However, consider performance: higher resolutions require more GPU power for gaming and may make text/UI elements very small, requiring scaling adjustments for comfortable viewing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Why does my 4K monitor look blurry on Windows?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "This is usually due to scaling issues. 4K monitors at 100% scaling make everything tiny, so Windows automatically scales to 150-200%. Some older applications don't handle scaling well. Try adjusting scaling in Display Settings or enable \"Fix scaling for apps\" in Windows 10/11."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use different resolutions on multiple monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, modern operating systems handle different resolutions well. However, mouse movement between screens might feel inconsistent, and window dragging can be awkward if resolutions differ significantly. For best experience, try to match resolutions or at least aspect ratios."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What resolution should I use for streaming or recording?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For streaming: 1080p 60fps is the most common. Use 720p 60fps for slower internet or 1440p if you have excellent upload speed. For recording: Use your native resolution for best quality, or 1080p for broader compatibility and smaller file sizes."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I change my resolution if I can't see the screen properly?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Boot into Safe Mode (Windows) or Recovery Mode (Mac) where lower resolutions are used by default. On Windows, you can also press Windows + P to cycle through display modes, or use Windows + I to open Settings and navigate to Display by keyboard. On Mac, reset NVRAM/PRAM by holding Option + Command + P + R during startup."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is higher resolution always better?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Not necessarily. Higher resolution provides more detail and screen space but requires more GPU power, makes text smaller (requiring scaling), and creates larger file sizes for content creation. The \"best\" resolution depends on your screen size, use case, graphics hardware, and viewing distance. ---"
      }
    },
    {

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the most common laptop screen size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "15.6 inches is the most common laptop screen size globally, accounting for approximately 40-50% of laptop sales. It offers the best balance between screen real estate, portability, cost, and component availability. The second most popular is 13.3-14 inches for ultraportable laptops."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure a laptop screen with a broken screen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "If the screen is broken but display boundaries are still visible, measure the diagonal of the visible display area (not the bezel). If completely shattered, check your laptop model number on the bottom sticker or search online for your model's specifications. The screen size is almost always listed in official specs."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does laptop screen size include the bezel?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No, laptop screen size does not include the bezel (frame). It only measures the active display area - the diagonal distance of the actual screen. This is why two laptops with the same screen size (e.g., 15.6\") can have different overall dimensions if one has thinner bezels."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I replace my laptop screen with a different size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generally no. Laptop screens are specifically designed for each model. The screen size, mounting points, connector type, and cable length are model-specific. However, you can sometimes find screens with the same physical size but different resolution or panel type (IPS vs. TN) that are compatible. Always verify compatibility before purchasing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between laptop screen size and resolution?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Screen size is the physical diagonal measurement in inches (e.g., 15.6\"). Resolution is the number of pixels (e.g., 1920×1080). They're independent - the same 15.6\" screen can have 1366×768 (HD), 1920×1080 (Full HD), or 3840×2160 (4K) resolution. Higher resolution means sharper image but requires more GPU power."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is a 15.6\" laptop too big to carry?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "It depends on your needs and tolerance. A 15.6\" laptop typically weighs 4-5.5 lbs (1.8-2.5 kg) and fits in most backpacks. For daily commuting or frequent travel, 13-14\" is generally more comfortable. For occasional carry between home and office, 15.6\" is manageable. Consider a lightweight 15.6\" model (under 4 lbs) if you need the screen space but want better portability."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure a MacBook screen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Measure exactly the same way as any laptop: diagonal distance from corner to corner of the active display area only, excluding the black bezel. MacBooks make this easy because the aluminum bezel is clearly distinct from the glass display. For recent models, the size is in the product name (MacBook Pro 14-inch, 16-inch, MacBook Air 13-inch)."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What laptop screen size is best for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "15.6\" to 17.3\" is ideal for gaming laptops. 15.6\" offers the best balance - still somewhat portable, fits gaming hardware, large enough for immersion. 17.3\" provides maximum immersion but sacrifices portability. Screens smaller than 15\" often can't fit high-performance gaming components. Also consider high refresh rate (144Hz+) which is more important than size for competitive gaming. ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What laptop screen size is best for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {

        "text": "15.6\" to 17.3\" is ideal for gaming laptops. 15.6\" offers the best balance - still somewhat portable, fits gaming hardware, large enough for immersion. 17.3\" provides maximum immersion but sacrifices portability.       }
    }
  ]
}

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I measure the screen or the bezel?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Only measure the screen, not the bezel (frame). The advertised monitor size refers only to the visible display area, excluding the plastic or metal frame around it."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is monitor size measured diagonally?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, always diagonally, from one corner of the screen to the diagonally opposite corner. This is the industry standard for all monitors and TVs."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure a curved monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Measure the straight-line diagonal distance, not following the curve. Use a ruler or tape measure and measure as if the screen were flat, from corner to corner in a straight line."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if my measurement is between standard sizes?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Round to the nearest standard size. For example: - 23.5\"-23.9\" = 24\" monitor - 27.5\"-28\" = 27\" monitor - 31.5\"-32.5\" = 32\" monitor Manufacturers sometimes specify 23.8\" or 27.5\", but these are marketed as 24\" and 27\" respectively."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use my phone to measure monitor size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, if you have a measuring app that uses your phone's camera and AR capabilities. Apps like \"Measure\" (iOS) or \"Measure\" (Android) can estimate dimensions. However, physical measurement is more accurate."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure an ultrawide monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Same method: Measure diagonally from corner to corner. Ultrawide monitors have larger diagonal measurements (34\", 38\", 49\") but the measurement technique is identical."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between monitor size and screen resolution?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Monitor size: Physical diagonal measurement (e.g., 27\") Screen resolution: Number of pixels (e.g., 2560×1440) They're independent: - Same size can have different resolutions - Same resolution can appear on different sizes"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How accurate does my measurement need to be?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Within ±0.5 inch is sufficient for most purposes. Manufacturers themselves have small variations (a \"27 inch\" might be 27.0\" or 27.5\"). Round to the nearest standard size for practical purposes. ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How accurate does my measurement need to be?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Within ±0.5 inch is sufficient for most purposes. Manufacturers themselves have small variations (a \"27 inch\" might be 27.0\" or 27.5\"). Round to the nearest standard size for practical purposes. ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}

      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For most gamers, 27 inches at 1440p is the sweet spot. It offers enough screen real estate for immersion without being too large to see the entire screen comfortably. For competitive esports, some prefer 24 inches at 1080p to see everything at a glance. If you sit further back or want maximum immersion, 32 inches at 4K works well."
      }
    },

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 4K worth it in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "It depends on your use case. For content creation, photo editing, and productivity with lots of text, 4K is excellent at 27\" or larger. For gaming, 4K requires a powerful GPU (RTX 4070 or better) to run smoothly at high refresh rates. For casual use and gaming on mid-range hardware, 1440p offers better value."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "IPS or VA for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "IPS is generally better for most gaming due to faster response times and better color accuracy. Choose VA if you primarily play single-player games, watch movies, or game in a dark room where the superior contrast is noticeable. Avoid VA for competitive multiplayer due to potential black smearing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much should I spend on a monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "A good rule is to spend 15-25% of your total PC budget on the monitor. For a $1000 PC, budget $150-250. For a $2000 PC, budget $300-500. Remember monitors last longer than other components, so it's worth investing in quality."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I need G-Sync or FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "FreeSync is free and works with both AMD and NVIDIA GPUs, making it the practical choice for most buyers. Hardware G-Sync (with the module) offers slightly better performance at the extreme low end of the refresh range, but the premium is only worth it for high-end setups with NVIDIA GPUs."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the best refresh rate for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "144Hz is the sweet spot for most gamers - it's a huge improvement over 60Hz and achievable with mid-range GPUs. 240Hz offers diminishing returns unless you play competitive esports. 60Hz is only acceptable for single-player games or if you have a very weak GPU."
      }
    },

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I buy a curved monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Curved monitors are personal preference. They can provide a more immersive experience for gaming and help reduce eye strain on ultrawide displays. config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Faut-il utiliser V-Sync avec G-Sync/FreeSync ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Dans le panneau NVIDIA : Activez (agit comme plafond d'images en haut de la plage VRR) Dans les jeux : Désactivez (évite le lag du double buffering) Cette combinaison donne le meilleur des deux mondes : pas de tearing dans la plage VRR et un plafonnement fluide au taux max."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Quel réglage de temps de réponse choisir ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Commencez à Moyen/Normal, puis testez avec testufo.com/ghosting. Augmentez si vous voyez des traînées. Diminuez si vous voyez du ghosting inversé (halos lumineux). Le \"1ms\" annoncé demande souvent un overdrive extrême qui cause des problèmes."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Plein écran ou fenêtré sans bordure ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Jeux compétitifs : Plein écran exclusif (input lag le plus bas) Casual/multitâche : Fenêtré sans bordure (alt-tab plus rapide) Note : Les optimisations de Windows 11 ont beaucoup réduit la différence."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Le HDR vaut-il le coup ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui si : - Votre écran est HDR600 ou mieux - Le jeu a un bon support HDR - Vous avez calibré les réglages HDR Non si : - L'écran est seulement HDR400 - Le HDR paraît délavé - Le jeu a une mauvaise implémentation HDR ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Le HDR vaut-il le coup ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui si : - Votre écran est HDR600 ou mieux - Le jeu a un bon support HDR - Vous avez calibré les réglages HDR Non si : - L'écran est seulement HDR400 - Le HDR paraît délavé - Le jeu a une mauvaise implémentation HDR ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

  "mainEntity": [

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 1440p better than 1080p for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For most new desktop setups, yes. 1440p is sharper and still realistic for high refresh. For competitive FPS, 1080p can still be better because it is easier to drive at very high frame rates."

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "FPS游戏24英寸还是27英寸更好？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24英寸更适合纯竞技FPS游戏（CS2、无畏契约），因为目标捕获更快，全屏周边视觉更好。27英寸更适合混合竞技和休闲FPS游戏的平衡游戏，提供更好的沉浸感同时保持竞技力。78%的职业FPS玩家使用24英寸显示器。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "为什么职业玩家使用小显示器？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "职业玩家使用24英寸显示器，因为他们可以在不移动眼睛或头部的情况下看到整个屏幕，从而获得15 25ms更快的反应时间。更小的屏幕允许更好的周边感知、更快的信息处理，并且是大多数电竞比赛中的锦标赛标准。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "32英寸对FPS游戏太大了吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "是的，32英寸对于竞技FPS游戏通常太大。研究表明，由于眼球移动过多，目标捕获比24英寸显示器慢25 35%。只有2%的职业FPS玩家使用32英寸显示器，而且主要是内容创作者。32英寸适合休闲FPS游戏和单机游戏。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "大多数FPS职业选手使用什么显示器尺寸？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "78%的职业FPS玩家使用24 24.5英寸显示器，20%使用27英寸显示器。最受欢迎的型号是明基ZOWIE XL2546K和华硕ROG Swift PG259QN。更大的显示器在竞技游戏中极为罕见。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "FPS游戏应该选1080p还是1440p？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24英寸竞技FPS：1080p 240Hz+最优，以获得最大性能。27英寸平衡游戏：1440p 165Hz+提供更好的视觉质量，没有重大性能损失。大多数职业选手使用1080p以获得竞技优势，而爱好者偏好1440p以获得图像质量。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "我应该坐在离游戏显示器多远？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24英寸：51 61厘米。27英寸：61 71厘米。32英寸：76 91厘米。坐得比最佳距离近会导致眼睛疲劳；坐得更远会降低竞技优势和沉浸感。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "我可以用电视玩FPS游戏吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "电视（40英寸以上）不推荐用于竞技FPS游戏，因为尺寸过大导致目标捕获缓慢，通常输入延迟更高（即使在游戏模式下），以及观看距离挑战。电视适合休闲沙发游戏，但在竞技游戏中处于显著劣势。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "FPS游戏的最佳刷新率是多少？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "最低：144Hz（现代标准）。推荐：240Hz（竞技最佳点）。职业：360Hz+（精英性能）。60Hz和144Hz之间的差异是巨大的；144Hz到240Hz是明显的；240Hz到360Hz+是微妙的，但在职业水平上可能很重要。"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "曲面还是平面显示器更适合游戏？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "这取决于游戏风格。平面显示器更适合竞技游戏（零失真、赛事标准、精确瞄准），而曲面显示器在沉浸式单机游戏中表现出色（更好的FOV、减少眼部疲劳、更电影化）。对于大多数玩家，平面27英寸显示器提供最佳多功能性。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "曲面显示器会导致眼部疲劳吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "不会，曲面显示器相比平面显示器通常减少眼部疲劳。曲线匹配自然眼部曲率，减少屏幕间眼部移动，保持一致观看距离。但是，一些用户需要1 2周时间来适应曲率。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "曲面显示器适合竞技游戏吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "一般不适合。98%以上的职业竞技玩家使用平面显示器，因为曲面显示器可能引入影响瞄准精度的微妙几何失真。赛事专用平面显示器，所以竞技玩家应该在平面显示器上训练以保持一致性。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "什么尺寸显示器应该是曲面vs平面？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27英寸：始终平面（曲率优势最小）。32英寸：任何都可以，轻微偏好曲面。34英寸超宽屏：强烈推荐曲面。49英寸超超宽屏：曲面必需。显示器越大，曲率越有益。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "曲面显示器会扭曲图像吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "是的，轻微。曲面显示器引入微妙几何失真，直线可能显示轻微弯曲，特别是在屏幕边缘。这在游戏中通常不明显，但可能影响生产力工作、网页浏览和竞技游戏精度。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "曲面显示器值得额外成本吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "仅在特定场景下：32英寸以上沉浸式游戏显示器、超宽屏显示器（必需）、模拟赛车设置。对于24 27英寸显示器或竞技游戏，溢价不合理。曲面为正确使用场景提供真正优势，但不是普遍更好。"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "2026 年 OLED 比 IPS 更适合游戏吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "纯游戏体验上，OLED 的动态清晰度、黑位和 HDR 更强。但如果你大量办公、写代码、长时间显示静态窗口，Fast IPS 更稳。"

        "@type": "Answer",

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "TN 还值得买吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "只在低预算电竞场景值得考虑。大多数用户应该先看 Fast IPS，预算充足且游戏优先再看 OLED。"
      }
    },

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "1440p 游戏显示器选什么面板？",

        "@type": "Answer",

      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "4K 游戏显示器选什么面板？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "高端游戏优先选 32 英寸 OLED 或 Mini LED；工作游戏混用选 27/32 英寸 IPS 或 Mini LED IPS。4K TN 不推荐。"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "超宽屏比双显示器更适合游戏吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "对于沉浸式单机游戏，是的——超宽屏提供无边框的无缝电影体验。对于竞技游戏和多任务处理，双显示器更好，因为16:9兼容性、用于Discord/指南的副屏幕和更高刷新率可用性。85%的游戏支持超宽屏，但竞技游戏通常不支持。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "我可以使用超宽屏显示器玩竞技FPS游戏吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "大多数竞技FPS游戏要么不支持超宽屏（CS2、无畏契约），要么额外的FOV被认为是劣势。99%的职业选手使用标准16:9显示器。超宽屏在锦标赛中被禁止。对于休闲竞技游戏，一些游戏如使命召唤和Apex有效，但你可能处于劣势。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "超宽屏游戏需要什么GPU？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "对于3440×1440（21:9）60 FPS：最低RTX 4060 Ti。144 FPS：RTX 4070或更好。对于5120×1440（32:9）60 FPS：RTX 4070。144 FPS：RTX 4080或4090。超宽屏需要比标准1440p多34 100%的GPU算力。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "2025年超宽屏显示器值得吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "是的，如果你主要玩单机游戏、从事内容创作或重视沉浸式体验。如果你玩竞技FPS、定期直播或需要真正的多任务处理，则不值得。考虑你的主要用例——超宽屏在特定任务中出色，但并非普遍更好。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "我应该买34英寸超宽屏还是双27英寸显示器？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "34英寸超宽屏如果：沉浸式游戏、视频编辑、编程、简洁美学。双27英寸如果：竞技游戏、直播、多任务处理、灵活性。预算：双27英寸可能更便宜（¥4900 vs ¥3500 5600）。性能：超宽屏需要更好的GPU。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "所有游戏都支持超宽屏显示器吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "不。约85%的现代3A游戏完全支持21:9。大多数竞技FPS不支持（CS2、无畏契约锁定为16:9）。32:9支持约45%。老游戏和一些主机移植缺乏支持。存在社区Mod但不推荐用于在线游戏。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "我可以将49英寸超宽屏用作两个显示器吗？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "是的，大多数49英寸超宽屏显示器有画中画（PbP）模式，将它们视为两个单独的显示器。但是，你会在中间有一个无边框分割。比物理边框好，但不是真正的双显示器。Windows根据模式将其视为一个或两个显示器。"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "哪个更适合生产力：超宽屏还是双显示器？",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "取决于工作流程。超宽屏更好：编程、视频编辑、无缝工作流程、简洁美学。双显示器更好：视频通话+工作、完全分离的应用程序、混合方向（横向+纵向）、窗口管理。最有生产力：49英寸超宽屏或三显示器，但对许多人来说过度。"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist 24 oder 27 Zoll besser für FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 Zoll ist besser für rein kompetitives FPS Gaming (CS2, Valorant) aufgrund schnellerer Zielerfassung und vollständigem peripherem Sichtfeld. 27 Zoll ist besser für ausgewogenes Gaming, das kompetitive und Casual FPS Titel mischt, und bietet bessere Immersion bei gleichzeitiger Wettbewerbsfähigkeit. 78% der FPS Profis verwenden 24 Zoll Monitore."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Warum verwenden Pro-Gamer kleine Monitore?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pro Gamer verwenden 24 Zoll Monitore, weil sie den gesamten Bildschirm ohne Augen oder Kopfbewegung sehen können, was zu 15 25ms schnelleren Reaktionszeiten führt.       "name": "Ist 32 Zoll zu groß für FPS-Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, 32 Zoll ist generell zu groß für kompetitives FPS Gaming. Studien zeigen, dass die Zielerfassung 25 35% langsamer ist im Vergleich zu 24 Zoll Monitoren aufgrund exzessiver Augenbewegung. Nur 2% der professionellen FPS Spieler verwenden 32 Zoll Displays, und das sind hauptsächlich Content Ersteller. 32\" ist in Ordnung für Casual FPS Gaming und Einzelspieler Titel."

      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich 1080p oder 1440p für FPS-Gaming nehmen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für 24 Zoll kompetitives FPS: 1080p bei 240Hz+ ist optimal für maximale Performance. Für 27 Zoll ausgewogenes Gaming: 1440p bei 165Hz+ bietet bessere visuelle Qualität ohne großen Performance Verlust. Die meisten Pro Spieler verwenden 1080p für kompetitiven Vorteil, während Enthusiasten 1440p für Bildqualität bevorzugen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Wie weit sollte ich von meinem Gaming-Monitor sitzen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für 24 Zoll: 51 61cm. Für 27 Zoll: 61 71cm. Für 32 Zoll: 76 91cm. Näher als optimal sitzen verursacht Augenbelastung; weiter sitzen reduziert kompetitiven Vorteil und Immersion."

        "@type": "Answer",

      "name": "Ist ein curved oder flat Monitor besser für Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Es hängt vom Gaming Stil ab. Flat Monitore sind besser für Competitive Gaming (null Verzerrung, Tournament Standard, präzises Zielen), während curved Monitore bei immersivem Einzelspieler Gaming glänzen (besseres FOV, reduzierte Augenbelastung, cinematischer). Für die meisten Gamer bieten flat 27\" Monitore die beste Vielseitigkeit."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Verursachen curved Monitore Augenbelastung?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Nein, curved Monitore reduzieren typischerweise Augenbelastung verglichen mit flat Monitoren. Die Kurve entspricht natürlicher Augenkrümmung, reduziert Augenbewegung über Bildschirm und behält konsistenten Betrachtungsabstand. Jedoch benötigen manche Nutzer 1 2 Wochen um sich an die Krümmung zu gewöhnen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sind curved Monitore gut für Competitive Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generell nein. 98%+ professioneller Competitive Gamer verwenden flat Monitore, da curved Monitore subtile Geometrie Verzerrung einführen können, die Ziel Präzision beeinflusst. Turniere verwenden ausschließlich flat Monitore, daher sollten competitive Spieler auf flat Displays trainieren für Konsistenz."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche Monitor-Größe sollte curved vs flat sein?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27\": Immer flat (Krümmungsvorteil minimal). 32\": Beides funktioniert, leichte Präferenz für curved. 34\" Ultrawide: Stark curved empfohlen. 49\" Super Ultrawide: Curved essentiell. Je größer der Monitor, desto vorteilhafter wird Krümmung."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Verzerren curved Monitore das Bild?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, leicht. Curved Monitore führen subtile geometrische Verzerrung ein, wo gerade Linien leicht gekrümmt erscheinen können, besonders an Bildschirmrändern. Dies ist meist nicht bemerkbar in Spielen, kann aber Produktivitätsarbeit, Web Browsing und Competitive Gaming Präzision beeinträchtigen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sind curved Monitore die zusätzlichen Kosten wert?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Nur in spezifischen Szenarien: 32\"+ Monitore für immersives Gaming, Ultrawide Monitore (essentiell), Sim Racing Setups. Für 24 27\" Monitore oder Competitive Gaming ist der Aufpreis nicht gerechtfertigt. Curved bietet echte Vorteile für richtige Anwendungsfälle, ist aber nicht universell besser."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

      "name": "Ist Ultrawide besser als Dual-Monitore für Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für immersives Einzelspieler Gaming, ja—Ultrawide bietet nahtloses, kinoreifes Erlebnis ohne Bezels. Für kompetitives Gaming und Multitasking sind Dual Monitore besser aufgrund 16:9 Kompatibilität, sekundärem Bildschirm für Discord/Guides und höherer Refresh Rate Verfügbarkeit. 85% der Spiele unterstützen Ultrawide, aber kompetitive Titel oft nicht."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Kann ich einen Ultrawide-Monitor für kompetitive FPS-Spiele nutzen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Die meisten kompetitiven FPS Spiele unterstützen entweder kein Ultrawide (CS2, Valorant) oder das extra FOV wird als Nachteil betrachtet. 99% der professionellen Spieler nutzen Standard 16:9 Monitore. Ultrawides sind Turnier verboten. Für casual kompetitives Spiel funktionieren manche Spiele wie COD und Apex, aber Sie könnten im Nachteil sein."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche GPU brauche ich für Ultrawide-Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für 3440×1440 (21:9) bei 60 FPS: RTX 4060 Ti minimum. Für 144 FPS: RTX 4070 oder besser. Für 5120×1440 (32:9) bei 60 FPS: RTX 4070. Für 144 FPS: RTX 4080 oder 4090. Ultrawide benötigt 34 100% mehr GPU Power als Standard 1440p."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sind Ultrawide-Monitore 2025 ihr Geld wert?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, wenn Sie primär Einzelspieler Spiele spielen, Content Erstellung betreiben oder immersive Erfahrungen schätzen. Nicht wert wenn Sie kompetitive FPS spielen, regelmäßig streamen oder echtes Multitasking brauchen. Betrachten Sie Ihren primären Anwendungsfall—Ultrawides glänzen bei spezifischen Aufgaben, sind aber nicht universell besser."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich einen 34\" Ultrawide oder Dual 27\" Monitore bekommen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "34\" Ultrawide wenn: immersives Gaming, Video Bearbeitung, Programmierung, saubere Ästhetik. Dual 27\" wenn: kompetitives Gaming, Streaming, Multitasking, Flexibilität. Budget: Dual 27\" können günstiger sein (€700 vs €500 800). Performance: Ultrawide braucht bessere GPU."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Unterstützen alle Spiele Ultrawide-Monitore?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Nein. Etwa 85% der modernen AAA Spiele unterstützen voll 21:9. Die meisten kompetitiven FPS nicht (CS2, Valorant locked auf 16:9). 32:9 Support ist ~45%. Ältere Spiele und manche Konsolen Ports fehlt Support. Community Mods existieren, sind aber nicht für Online Spiele empfohlen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welches ist besser für Produktivität: Ultrawide oder Dual-Monitore?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Hängt vom Workflow ab. Ultrawide besser für: Coding, Video Bearbeitung, nahtlose Workflows, saubere Ästhetik. Dual Monitore besser für: Video Calls + Arbeit, komplett separate Anwendungen, gemischte Orientierungen (Landscape + Portrait), Fenster Management. Am produktivsten: 49\" Ultrawide oder Triple Monitore, aber Overkill für viele."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Es mejor 24 o 27 pulgadas para FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 pulgadas es mejor para FPS gaming puramente competitivo (CS2, Valorant) debido a adquisición de objetivos más rápida y visión periférica de pantalla completa. 27 pulgadas es mejor para gaming balanceado que mezcla títulos FPS competitivos y casuales, ofreciendo mejor inmersión mientras permanece competitivo. 78% de jugadores FPS pro usan monitores de 24 pulgadas."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Por qué los gamers pro usan monitores pequeños?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Los gamers pro usan monitores de 24 pulgadas porque pueden ver toda la pantalla sin movimiento de ojos o cabeza, resultando en tiempos de reacción 15 25ms más rápidos. Pantallas más pequeñas permiten mejor conciencia periférica, procesamiento de información más rápido, y son el estándar de torneo en la mayoría de competiciones de esports."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Es 32 pulgadas demasiado grande para FPS gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sí, 32 pulgadas es generalmente demasiado grande para FPS gaming competitivo. Los estudios muestran que la adquisición de objetivos es 25 35% más lenta comparado con monitores de 24 pulgadas debido a movimiento ocular excesivo. Solo 2% de jugadores FPS profesionales usan displays de 32 pulgadas, y esos son principalmente creadores de contenido. 32\" está bien para FPS casual y títulos para un jugador."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Qué tamaño de monitor usan la mayoría de pros de FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "78% de jugadores FPS profesionales usan monitores de 24 24.5 pulgadas, mientras que 20% usan displays de 27 pulgadas. Los modelos más populares son BenQ ZOWIE XL2546K y ASUS ROG Swift PG259QN. Monitores más grandes son extremadamente raros en juego competitivo."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Debería obtener 1080p o 1440p para FPS gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para FPS competitivo de 24 pulgadas: 1080p a 240Hz+ es óptimo para máximo rendimiento. Para gaming balanceado de 27 pulgadas: 1440p a 165Hz+ ofrece mejor calidad visual sin pérdida mayor de rendimiento. La mayoría de jugadores pro usan 1080p por ventaja competitiva, mientras entusiastas prefieren 1440p por calidad de imagen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Qué tan lejos debería sentarme de mi monitor gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para 24 pulgadas: 51 61cm. Para 27 pulgadas: 61 71cm. Para 32 pulgadas: 76 91cm. Sentarse más cerca que lo óptimo causa fatiga ocular; sentarse más lejos reduce ventaja competitiva e inmersión."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Cuál es la mejor tasa de refresco para FPS gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Mínimo: 144Hz (estándar moderno). Recomendado: 240Hz (punto óptimo competitivo). Profesional: 360Hz+ (rendimiento elite). La diferencia entre 60Hz y 144Hz es dramática; 144Hz a 240Hz es notable; 240Hz a 360Hz+ es sutil pero puede importar en niveles profesionales."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Es un monitor curvo o plano mejor para gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Depende del estilo gaming. Los monitores planos son mejores para gaming competitivo (cero distorsión, estándar torneo, puntería precisa), mientras los monitores curvos sobresalen en gaming inmersivo un jugador (mejor FOV, fatiga ocular reducida, más cinemático). Para la mayoría de gamers, los monitores planos 27\" ofrecen la mejor versatilidad."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Los monitores curvos causan fatiga ocular?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No, los monitores curvos típicamente reducen fatiga ocular comparado con monitores planos. La curva coincide con curvatura ocular natural, reduce movimiento ocular a través pantalla y mantiene distancia visualización consistente. Sin embargo, algunos usuarios necesitan 1 2 semanas para adaptarse a la curvatura inicialmente."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Son buenos los monitores curvos para gaming competitivo?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generalmente no. 98%+ de gamers competitivos profesionales usan monitores planos porque los monitores curvos pueden introducir distorsión geométrica sutil que afecta precisión puntería. Los torneos usan exclusivamente monitores planos, así que jugadores competitivos deberían entrenar en displays planos para consistencia."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Qué tamaño monitor debería ser curvo vs plano?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27\": Siempre plano (beneficio curvatura mínimo). 32\": Cualquiera funciona, preferencia leve por curvo. 34\" ultrawide: Fuertemente recomendado curvo. 49\" super ultrawide: Curvo esencial. Mientras más grande el monitor, más beneficiosa se vuelve la curvatura."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Los monitores curvos distorsionan la imagen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sí, ligeramente. Los monitores curvos introducen distorsión geométrica sutil donde líneas rectas pueden aparecer ligeramente curvadas, especialmente en bordes pantalla. Esto usualmente no es notable en juegos pero puede afectar trabajo productividad, navegación web y precisión gaming competitivo."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Valen los monitores curvos el costo extra?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Solo en escenarios específicos: monitores 32\"+ para gaming inmersivo, monitores ultrawide (esencial), setups sim racing. Para monitores 24 27\" o gaming competitivo, la prima no está justificada. Curvo proporciona beneficios reales para casos uso correctos pero no es universalmente mejor."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Es ultrawide mejor que monitores duales para gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para gaming inmersivo de un jugador, sí—ultrawide proporciona experiencia cinemática continua sin biseles. Para gaming competitivo y multitarea, los monitores duales son mejores debido a compatibilidad 16:9, pantalla secundaria para Discord/guías y disponibilidad de mayor tasa de refresco. 85% de juegos soportan ultrawide, pero títulos competitivos a menudo no."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Puedo usar un monitor ultrawide para juegos FPS competitivos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "La mayoría de juegos FPS competitivos o no soportan ultrawide (CS2, Valorant) o el FOV extra se considera una desventaja. 99% de jugadores profesionales usan monitores 16:9 estándar. Los ultrawides están prohibidos en torneos. Para juego competitivo casual, algunos juegos como COD y Apex funcionan, pero puedes estar en desventaja."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Qué GPU necesito para gaming ultrawide?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para 3440×1440 (21:9) a 60 FPS: RTX 4060 Ti mínimo. Para 144 FPS: RTX 4070 o mejor. Para 5120×1440 (32:9) a 60 FPS: RTX 4070. Para 144 FPS: RTX 4080 o 4090. Ultrawide requiere 34 100% más poder de GPU que 1440p estándar."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Valen la pena los monitores ultrawide en 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sí, si principalmente juegas juegos de un jugador, haces creación de contenido o valoras experiencias inmersivas. No vale la pena si juegas FPS competitivos, haces stream regularmente o necesitas verdadera multitarea. Considera tu caso de uso principal—los ultrawides sobresalen en tareas específicas pero no son universalmente mejores."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Debería obtener un 34\" ultrawide o monitores duales 27\"?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "34\" ultrawide si: gaming inmersivo, edición de video, programación, estética limpia. Dual 27\" si: gaming competitivo, streaming, multitarea, flexibilidad. Presupuesto: dual 27\" puede ser más barato ($700 vs $500 800). Rendimiento: ultrawide necesita mejor GPU."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Todos los juegos soportan monitores ultrawide?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No. Aproximadamente 85% de juegos AAA modernos soportan completamente 21:9. La mayoría de FPS competitivos no (CS2, Valorant bloqueado a 16:9). Soporte 32:9 es ~45%. Juegos antiguos y algunos ports de consola carecen de soporte. Existen mods de comunidad pero no se recomiendan para juegos en línea."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "¿Cuál es mejor para productividad: ultrawide o monitores duales?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Depende del flujo de trabajo. Ultrawide mejor para: coding, edición de video, flujos de trabajo continuos, estética limpia. Monitores duales mejor para: video llamadas + trabajo, aplicaciones completamente separadas, orientaciones mixtas (horizontal + vertical), gestión de ventanas. Más productivo: 49\" ultrawide o monitores triples, pero excesivo para muchos."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "24 ou 27 polegadas é melhor para FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 polegadas é melhor para jogos FPS puramente competitivos (CS2, Valorant) devido à aquisição de alvos mais rápida e visão periférica de tela cheia. 27 polegadas é melhor para jogos equilibrados que misturam títulos FPS competitivos e casuais, oferecendo melhor imersão enquanto permanecem competitivos. 78% dos jogadores profissionais de FPS usam monitores de 24 polegadas."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Por que os jogadores profissionais usam monitores pequenos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Jogadores profissionais usam monitores de 24 polegadas porque podem ver a tela inteira sem movimento ocular ou de cabeça, resultando em tempos de reação 15 25ms mais rápidos. Telas menores permitem melhor consciência periférica, processamento de informações mais rápido e são o padrão de torneio na maioria das competições de esports."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "32 polegadas é muito grande para jogos FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sim, 32 polegadas é geralmente muito grande para jogos FPS competitivos. Estudos mostram que a aquisição de alvos é 25 35% mais lenta em comparação com monitores de 24 polegadas devido ao movimento ocular excessivo. Apenas 2% dos jogadores profissionais de FPS usam telas de 32 polegadas, e esses são principalmente criadores de conteúdo. 32\" é bom para jogos FPS casuais e títulos single player."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Qual tamanho de monitor a maioria dos profissionais de FPS usa?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "78% dos jogadores profissionais de FPS usam monitores de 24 24.5 polegadas, enquanto 20% usam telas de 27 polegadas. Os modelos mais populares são BenQ ZOWIE XL2546K e ASUS ROG Swift PG259QN. Monitores maiores são extremamente raros no jogo competitivo."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Devo pegar 1080p ou 1440p para jogos FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para FPS competitivo de 24 polegadas: 1080p a 240Hz+ é ideal para desempenho máximo. Para jogos equilibrados de 27 polegadas: 1440p a 165Hz+ oferece melhor qualidade visual sem grande perda de desempenho. A maioria dos jogadores profissionais usa 1080p para vantagem competitiva, enquanto entusiastas preferem 1440p para qualidade de imagem."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "A que distância devo sentar do meu monitor gamer?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para 24 polegadas: 20 24 polegadas (51 61cm). Para 27 polegadas: 24 28 polegadas (61 71cm). Para 32 polegadas: 30 36 polegadas (76 91cm). Sentar mais perto do que o ideal causa tensão ocular; sentar mais longe reduz vantagem competitiva e imersão."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Posso usar uma TV para jogos FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "TVs (40\"+) não são recomendadas para jogos FPS competitivos devido ao tamanho excessivo causando aquisição de alvos lenta, tipicamente maior latência de entrada (mesmo no modo jogo) e desafios de distância de visualização. TVs são aceitáveis para jogos casuais no sofá, mas colocam você em desvantagem significativa no jogo competitivo."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Qual é a melhor taxa de atualização para jogos FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Mínimo: 144Hz (padrão moderno). Recomendado: 240Hz (ponto ideal competitivo). Profissional: 360Hz+ (desempenho de elite). A diferença entre 60Hz e 144Hz é dramática; 144Hz para 240Hz é perceptível; 240Hz para 360Hz+ é sutil, mas pode importar em níveis profissionais."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Um monitor curvo ou plano é melhor para jogos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Depende do estilo de jogo. Monitores planos são melhores para jogos competitivos (zero distorção, padrão de torneio, mira precisa), enquanto monitores curvos se destacam em jogos imersivos single player (melhor FOV, tensão ocular reduzida, mais cinemático). Para a maioria dos jogadores, monitores planos de 27\" oferecem a melhor versatilidade."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Monitores curvos causam tensão ocular?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Não, monitores curvos tipicamente reduzem a tensão ocular comparados aos monitores planos. A curva combina com a curvatura natural do olho, reduz o movimento ocular através da tela e mantém a distância de visualização consistente. No entanto, alguns usuários precisam de 1 2 semanas para se adaptar à curvatura inicialmente."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Monitores curvos são bons para jogos competitivos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Geralmente não. 98%+ dos jogadores competitivos profissionais usam monitores planos porque monitores curvos podem introduzir distorção geométrica sutil que afeta a precisão da mira. Torneios usam exclusivamente monitores planos, então jogadores competitivos devem treinar em telas planas para consistência."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Qual tamanho de monitor deve ser curvo vs plano?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27\": Sempre plano (benefício da curvatura mínimo). 32\": Qualquer um funciona, leve preferência por curvo. 34\" ultrawide: Fortemente recomendado curvo. 49\" super ultrawide: Essencial curvo. Quanto maior o monitor, mais benéfica a curvatura se torna."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Monitores curvos distorcem a imagem?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sim, levemente. Monitores curvos introduzem distorção geométrica sutil onde linhas retas podem parecer levemente curvas, especialmente nas bordas da tela. Isso geralmente não é perceptível em jogos, mas pode afetar trabalho de produtividade, navegação na web e precisão de jogos competitivos."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Monitores curvos valem o custo extra?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Apenas em cenários específicos: monitores de 32\"+ para jogos imersivos, monitores ultrawide (essencial), configurações de sim racing. Para monitores de 24 27\" ou jogos competitivos, o prêmio não é justificado. Curvo fornece benefícios reais para os casos de uso certos, mas não é universalmente melhor."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Você pode montar monitores curvos na parede?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sim, mas com limitações. Monitores curvos não ficam nivelados contra paredes e requerem mais espaço de profundidade. Braços de montagem especiais podem ser necessários. Monitores planos são muito mais fáceis de montar na parede e mais eficientes em espaço."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Streamers preferem monitores curvos ou planos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "A maioria dos streamers prefere monitores planos porque gravam melhor na câmera, fornecem perspectiva padrão do espectador e oferecem melhor versatilidade para vários tipos de conteúdo. Monitores curvos podem criar distorção de vídeo e são mais difíceis de enquadrar corretamente para configurações de streaming."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ultrawide é melhor que monitores duplos para jogos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para jogos single player imersivos, sim — ultrawide fornece experiência contínua e cinemática sem molduras. Para jogos competitivos e multitarefa, monitores duplos são melhores devido à compatibilidade 16:9, tela secundária para Discord/guias e maior disponibilidade de taxa de atualização. 85% dos jogos suportam ultrawide, mas títulos competitivos frequentemente não."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Posso usar um monitor ultrawide para jogos FPS competitivos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "A maioria dos jogos FPS competitivos não suporta ultrawide (CS2, Valorant) ou o FOV extra é considerado uma desvantagem. 99% dos jogadores profissionais usam monitores 16:9 padrão. Ultrawides são banidos em torneios. Para jogo competitivo casual, alguns jogos como COD e Apex funcionam, mas você pode estar em desvantagem."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Qual GPU eu preciso para jogos ultrawide?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Para 3440×1440 (21:9) a 60 FPS: RTX 4060 Ti mínima. Para 144 FPS: RTX 4070 ou melhor. Para 5120×1440 (32:9) a 60 FPS: RTX 4070. Para 144 FPS: RTX 4080 ou 4090. Ultrawide requer 34 100% mais potência de GPU do que 1440p padrão."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Monitores ultrawide valem a pena em 2026?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sim, se você joga principalmente jogos single player, faz criação de conteúdo ou valoriza experiências imersivas. Não vale a pena se você joga FPS competitivo, transmite regularmente ou precisa de multitarefa verdadeira. Considere seu caso de uso principal — ultrawides se destacam em tarefas específicas, mas não são universalmente melhores."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Devo pegar um ultrawide de 34\" ou dois monitores de 27\"?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "34\" ultrawide se: jogos imersivos, edição de vídeo, programação, estética limpa. Duplo 27\" se: jogos competitivos, streaming, multitarefa, flexibilidade. Orçamento: duplo 27\" pode ser mais barato ($700 vs $500 800). Desempenho: ultrawide precisa de melhor GPU."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Todos os jogos suportam monitores ultrawide?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Não. Cerca de 85% dos jogos AAA modernos suportam totalmente 21:9. A maioria dos FPS competitivos não (CS2, Valorant bloqueados em 16:9). Suporte 32:9 é ~45%. Jogos mais antigos e alguns ports de console carecem de suporte. Mods da comunidade existem, mas não são recomendados para jogos online."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Posso usar um ultrawide de 49\" como dois monitores?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Sim, a maioria dos monitores ultrawide de 49\" tem modo Picture by Picture (PbP) que os trata como dois displays separados. No entanto, você terá uma divisão sem moldura no meio. Melhor que moldura física, mas não monitores duplos verdadeiros. O Windows trata como um ou dois displays dependendo do modo."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Qual é melhor para produtividade: ultrawide ou monitores duplos?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Depende do fluxo de trabalho. Ultrawide melhor para: codificação, edição de vídeo, fluxos de trabalho contínuos, estética limpa. Monitores duplos melhores para: chamadas de vídeo + trabalho, aplicações completamente separadas, orientações mistas (paisagem + retrato), gerenciamento de janelas. Mais produtivo: 49\" ultrawide ou monitores triplos, mas exagero para muitos."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "24 ou 27 pouces pour le FPS ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Le 24 pouces est mieux pour le FPS compétitif pur (CS2, Valorant) grâce à une acquisition de cible plus rapide et une vision périphérique complète de l'écran. Le 27 pouces est mieux pour le gaming équilibré qui mixe compétitif et casual, avec plus d'immersion tout en restant compétitif. 78% des pros FPS jouent en 24 pouces."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Pourquoi les pros utilisent-ils de petits écrans ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Les pros jouent en 24 pouces parce qu'ils voient l'écran entier sans mouvement des yeux ou de la tête, ce qui donne des temps de réaction 15 25ms plus rapides. Les petits écrans permettent une meilleure conscience périphérique et un traitement de l'information plus rapide."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Le 32 pouces est-il trop grand pour le FPS ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui, le 32 pouces est généralement trop grand pour le FPS compétitif. Les études montrent que l'acquisition de cible est 25 35% plus lente par rapport aux écrans 24 pouces. Seulement 2% des pros FPS jouent en 32\". Le 32\" convient pour le FPS casual et les jeux solo."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Quel taux de rafraîchissement pour le FPS ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Minimum : 144Hz (standard moderne). Recommandé : 240Hz (sweet spot compétitif). Professionnel : 360Hz+ (performance élite). La différence entre 60Hz et 144Hz est spectaculaire ; entre 144Hz et 240Hz elle est visible ; entre 240Hz et 360Hz+ elle est subtile mais peut compter au niveau pro."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Puis-je utiliser une TV pour le FPS ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Les TV (40\"+) ne sont pas recommandées pour le FPS compétitif à cause de leur taille excessive qui ralentit l'acquisition de cible, d'un input lag généralement plus élevé (même en mode jeu), et des défis de distance de visionnage. Les TV conviennent pour le gaming casual canapé mais vous désavantagent en compétitif."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Un écran incurvé ou plat est-il meilleur pour le gaming ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ça dépend du style de jeu. Les écrans plats sont meilleurs pour le compétitif (zéro distorsion, standard tournoi, visée précise), tandis que les incurvés excellent en gaming solo immersif (meilleur FOV, moins de fatigue oculaire, plus cinématique). Pour la plupart des joueurs, un écran plat 27\" offre la meilleure polyvalence."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Les écrans incurvés causent-ils de la fatigue oculaire ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Non, les écrans incurvés réduisent généralement la fatigue oculaire par rapport aux plats. La courbe correspond à la courbure naturelle de l'œil, réduit le mouvement oculaire et maintient une distance de visionnage constante. Certains utilisateurs ont besoin de 1 2 semaines pour s'adapter à la courbure."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Les écrans incurvés sont-ils bons pour le gaming compétitif ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "En général non. 98%+ des joueurs compétitifs professionnels utilisent des écrans plats car les incurvés peuvent introduire une distorsion géométrique subtile qui affecte la précision de visée. Les tournois utilisent exclusivement des écrans plats."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Quelle taille d'écran devrait être incurvée vs plate ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27\" : Toujours plat (bénéfice de la courbure minimal). 32\" : Les deux marchent, légère préférence incurvé. 34\" ultrawide : Incurvé fortement recommandé. 49\" super ultrawide : Incurvé indispensable. Plus l'écran est grand, plus la courbure est bénéfique."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Les écrans incurvés déforment-ils l'image ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui, légèrement. Les écrans incurvés introduisent une distorsion géométrique subtile où les lignes droites peuvent paraître légèrement courbées, surtout aux bords. C'est généralement invisible en jeu mais peut affecter le travail de productivité et la précision en compétitif."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Les écrans incurvés valent-ils le surcoût ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Seulement dans des cas précis : écrans 32\"+ pour le gaming immersif, écrans ultrawide (indispensable), setups sim racing. Pour les 24 27\" ou le compétitif, le surcoût n'est pas justifié."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Peut-on fixer un écran incurvé au mur ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui, mais avec des limites. Les écrans incurvés ne se plaquent pas contre le mur et demandent plus de dégagement en profondeur. Des bras de support spéciaux peuvent être nécessaires. Les écrans plats sont bien plus faciles à fixer au mur."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Les streamers préfèrent-ils l'incurvé ou le plat ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "La plupart des streamers préfèrent les écrans plats car ils rendent mieux à la caméra, offrent une perspective standard pour les viewers et sont plus polyvalents pour différents types de contenu."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "L'ultrawide est-il meilleur que le double écran pour le gaming ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pour le gaming solo immersif, oui — l'ultrawide offre une expérience cinématique sans bordure. Pour le compétitif et le multitâche, le double écran est meilleur grâce à la compatibilité 16:9, l'écran secondaire pour Discord/guides et la disponibilité du high refresh. 85% des jeux supportent l'ultrawide, mais les titres compétitifs souvent non."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Peut-on utiliser un ultrawide pour les FPS compétitifs ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "La plupart des FPS compétitifs ne supportent pas l'ultrawide (CS2, Valorant) ou le FOV supplémentaire est considéré comme un désavantage. 99% des joueurs professionnels utilisent des écrans 16:9 standard. Les ultrawides sont interdits en tournoi."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Quel GPU faut-il pour le gaming ultrawide ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pour du 3440×1440 (21:9) à 60 FPS : RTX 4060 Ti minimum. Pour 144 FPS : RTX 4070 ou mieux. Pour du 5120×1440 (32:9) à 60 FPS : RTX 4070. Pour 144 FPS : RTX 4080 ou 4090. L'ultrawide demande 34 100% de puissance GPU en plus que le 1440p standard."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Faut-il un 34\" ultrawide ou deux 27\" ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "34\" ultrawide si : gaming immersif, montage vidéo, programmation, esthétique propre. Double 27\" si : gaming compétitif, streaming, multitâche, flexibilité. Budget : le double 27\" peut être moins cher (700$ vs 500 800$). Performance : l'ultrawide demande un meilleur GPU."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Tous les jeux supportent-ils l'ultrawide ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Non. Environ 85% des jeux AAA modernes supportent pleinement le 21:9. La plupart des FPS compétitifs ne le supportent pas (CS2, Valorant bloqués en 16:9). Le support 32:9 est à ~45%. Les anciens jeux et certains ports console manquent de support."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Peut-on utiliser un 49\" ultrawide comme deux écrans ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui, la plupart des 49\" ultrawide ont un mode Picture by Picture (PbP) qui les traite comme deux écrans séparés. Vous aurez une séparation sans bordure au milieu. Mieux qu'une bordure physique mais pas un vrai double écran."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Lequel est meilleur pour la productivité ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ça dépend du workflow. Ultrawide meilleur pour : code, montage vidéo, workflows sans coupure, esthétique propre. Double écran meilleur pour : visios + travail, applications complètement séparées, orientations mixtes (paysage + portrait), gestion de fenêtres."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>
